hotspots.title: Trump’s WHCD evacuation after shooting
hotspots.blurb: President Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after gunshots were heard, with a suspect in custody. Multiple news outlets covered the incident, including live updates and Trump’s subsequent address.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the high-profile nature of the event, the involvement of the President, and the immediate security concerns it raises.

hotspots.title: PP-Vox ‘national priority’ pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain’s PP and Vox have formalized regional pacts in Extremadura and Aragón, prioritizing Spanish nationals for public aid and housing. The agreements have sparked legal and moral debates, with opposition parties and the Church condemning the policies as discriminatory.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its moral and legal implications, polarized political reactions, and the broader debate over immigration and national identity.

hotspots.title: Orbán’s exit and Fidesz’s future in Hungary
hotspots.blurb: Viktor Orbán has stepped down from Hungary’s parliament following his party’s landslide defeat in the recent elections. Orbán will focus on reorganizing Fidesz, while the opposition Tisza Party, led by Péter Magyar, forms a new government.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to Orbán’s long-standing influence in Hungarian and European politics, the dramatic shift in power, and the uncertainty surrounding Fidesz’s future.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise and democratic backlash in Germany
hotspots.blurb: Germany’s far-right AfD has surged to a record 28% in polls, surpassing traditional parties. The party faces internal controversies, including the expulsion of a member for a Hitler-style speech and accusations of extremism.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the AfD’s rapid rise, its extremist rhetoric, and the broader implications for German democracy and European politics.

hotspots.title: Tisza Party’s cabinet formation in Hungary
hotspots.blurb: The Tisza Party, led by Péter Magyar, has begun forming Hungary’s new government following its electoral victory. Key appointments include Anita Orbán as Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister, with a focus on securing EU funds and addressing corruption.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the dramatic shift in Hungarian politics, the new government’s policy priorities, and the potential impact on Hungary’s relationship with the EU.
